title: Maranta arundastrum

The Maranta arundinacea family is a large and diverse group of plants, including over 31 genera. uses: Maranta arundinacea, commonly known as West Indian arrowroot or Maranta arundastrum, is a plant with various medicinal uses. Traditionally, it has been used to treat digestive issues and is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's rhizomes and roots are used to make a powder or extract that can be consumed to alleviate symptoms of diarrhea and constipation. Additionally, it has been used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its soothing and calming effects. Furthermore, the plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections and promote wound healing.

side_effects: Maranta arundinacea, also known as West Indian joy or arum lily, has been used in traditional medicine, but there is limited information available on its side effects.
